perturbation.original_text: Owner shall pay Service Provider within thirty (30) days after the invoice date. Fees are conditioned upon timely payment and any past due balance will accrue interest at the monthly rate of one and one half percent (1.5%).
perturbation.changed_text: Owner shall pay Service Provider within sixty (60) days after the invoice date. Fees are conditioned upon timely payment and any past due balance will accrue interest at the monthly rate of one and one half percent (1.5%).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days. This conflicts with Exhibit D, where the 'Services Fee' payment schedule is mentioned without specific time for payment, but is intended to mean by default as 'thirty (30) days' as per section 2.3. This creates ambiguity regarding the payment deadline and uncertainty about enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PAYMENT FREQUENCY INITIAL ANNUAL SERVICES FEE (AS MARKED) o QUARTERLY $41,000 þ SEMI-ANNUALLY o ANNUALLY 1. Service Provider Employees billed at $[*] per hour. 2. Subcontractor charges will be billed at cost plus [*] percent ([*]%). 3. Materials, travel, lodging and other expenses will be billed at direct cost plus [*] percent ([*]%). 4. Hourly Rates listed will escalate at a rate of [*]% per year beginning at the first anniversary of the Services Commencement Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 30 to 60 days, which directly contradicts the implied 30-day payment term in the contradicted text (Exhibit D) for 'Services Fee' as per section 2.3 (as per the Perturbation Explanation). This creates a clear inconsistency, as the contradicted text lacks a specific payment timeline, but is intended to default to the original 30-day term. The new 60-day term creates ambiguity about the enforceable payment deadline. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections now provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Services Commencement Date and remain effective for ten (10) years (the "Initial Term") unless terminated in accordance with its terms. This Agreement shall be subject to an automatic extension for consecutive one (1) year periods thereafter (each, an "Extension Term" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term"), unless terminated (i) in accordance with its terms or (ii) upon thirty (30) days' written notice by either Party to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Services Commencement Date and remain effective for five (5) years (the "Initial Term") unless terminated in accordance with its terms. This Agreement shall be subject to an automatic extension for consecutive one (1) year periods thereafter (each, an "Extension Term" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term"), unless terminated (i) in accordance with its terms or (ii) upon thirty (30) days' written notice by either Party to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the 'Initial Term' from ten (10) years to five (5) years causes a direct in-text contradiction with Section 3.2, which discusses obligations following the termination or expiration of 'this Agreement'. This section inherently references the original ten (10) year term because it forms the basis of expectations for post-termination actions, thus the change causes an inconsistency about the effective duration of those obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within five (5) days after the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and upon Owner's payment in full of the amounts due Service Provider under the Agreement, Service Provider shall deliver to Owner all of the SEF O&M Reports, SEF books, records and property in its possession or under its control, and all materials, supplies, consumables, manuals and any other items furnished to Service Provider by Owner.
perturbation.justification: The modification of the 'Initial Term' from ten (10) years to five (5) years directly contradicts the expectations set in the 'Contradicted Text', which specifies actions to be taken within five (5) days after the termination or expiration of the agreement. If the Initial Term is only five years, the basis of 'expiration of this agreement' as it relates to the 'Contradicted Text' becomes inconsistent and creates a contradiction about the effective duration of those obligations. The original ten (10) year term is inherently referenced when determining post-termination actions and deadlines, and therefore changing it causes a direct in-text contradiction as per Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Owner and Service Provider each acknowledge that User shall have no obligation to pay any amounts whatsoever under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner and Service Provider each acknowledge that User shall have full obligation to pay any amounts whatsoever under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the responsibility of payment from 'Owner' to 'User' introduce a contradiction. This contradicts the obligations outlined in Exhibit C-3 where it is stated Service Provider will 'ARRANGE DELIVERY OF ENERGY OUTPUT TO USER AND ANY INSTALLATION AND OPERATION OF EQUIPMENT ON USER'S SIDE NECESSARY FOR ACCEPTANCE AND USE OF THE ENERGY OUTPUT [SOURCE PPA 7(C)]', implicitly stating the user is the one that pays.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROVIDE AND TAKE REASONABLE MEASURES FOR SECURITY OF THE GENERATING FACILITY AGAINST ACCESS BY UNAUTHORIZED PERSONS, INCLUDING REASONABLE SECURITY FENCING IF APPROPRIATE [SOURCE: PPA 12(A)] RESPONSIBLE FOR THE IDENTIFICATION, CLEANUP, REMOVAL, REMEDIATION AND DISPOSAL OF HAZARDOUS MATERIALS USED, GENERATED, TREATED, STORED OR TRANSPORTED TO THE PREMISES. [SOURCE: PPA 13(E)] MAINTAIN COMPLETE AND ACCURATE RECORDS ON ALL MATTERS RELATING TO THE SEF AND MAINTAIN DATA AS MAY BE NECESSARY TO DETERMINE WITH REASONABLE ACCURACY ANY ITEM RELEVANT TO THE PPA. [SOURCE: PPA 17] TAKE GOOD CARE OF THE SEF; KEEP THE SAME IN GOOD ORDER AND CONDITION; AND MAKE AND PERFORM ALL REPAIRS. ALL REPAIRS SHALL BE AT LEAST EQUAL IN QUALITY AND COST TO THE ORIGINAL IMPROVEMENTS AND SHALL BE MADE IN ACCORDANCE WITH ALL LEGAL REQUIREMENTS, AND THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE PPA AND EASEMENT. THE NECESSITY FOR OR ADEQUACY OF REPAIRS SHALL BE MEASURED BY THE STANDARDS WHICH ARE APPROPRIATE FOR IMPROVEMENTS OF SIMILAR CONSTRUCTION AND CLASS, PROVIDED THAT SERVICE PROVIDER SHALL IN ANY EVENT MAKE ALL REPAIRS REASONABLY NECESSARY TO AVOID ANY STRUCTURAL DAMAGE OR OTHER DAMAGE OR INJURY TO THE SEF. [SOURCE: LEASE 5.02] OPERATE THE SEF AS REQUIRED UNDER THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND CASH GRANT GUIDANCE IN ORCDER TO MAINTAIN THE ELIGIBILITY OF THE SEF FOR ENERGY CREDITS UNDER SECTION 48 OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OR APPLICABLE CASG GRANTS UNDER SECTION 1603 OF THE AMERICAN RECOVERY AND REINVESTMENT ACT OF 2009 [SOURCE: STP1 OA, 4.01(Z), 4.02(W)] OBTAIN AND MAINTAIN IN GOOD STANDING ALL PERMITS, LICENSES AND GOVERNMENTAL APPROVALS NECESSARY FOR THE OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE OF THE SEF. [SOURCE: STP1 OA 4.02(B)] PROVIDE OWNER WITH SUCH INFORMATION AS NECSSARY FOR OWNER AND MASTER TENANT TO MAKE TIMELY, ACCURATE AND COMPLETE SUBMISSIONS OF REPORTS TO GOVERNMENTAL AGENCIES RELATED TO THE OPERATIONOR MAINTENANCE OF THE SEF. [SOURCE: STP1 OA 4.02 (G)] COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF ALL APPLICABLE LAWS IN THE OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE OF THE SEF, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, ALL STATE AND LOCAL ZONING LAWS, BUILDING CODES, HEALTH AND SAFETY CODES AND ALL OTHER GOVERNMENTAL OBLIGATIONS, AND CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS IDENTIFIED TO SERVICE PROVIDER. [SOURCE: STP1 OA 4.02(H)] PROVIDE OWNER AND MASTER TENANT OF NOTICE OF ANY WRITTEN OR ORAL NOTICE OF ANY DEFAULT OF FAILURE OF COMPLIANCE; LITIGATION OR CRIMINAL ACTION OR ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS, OR COMMUNICATION FROM ANY LENDER OR OTHER PERSON OR GOVERNMENTAL AUTHORITY WHICH IS NOT IN THE ORDINARY COURSE OF BUSINESS, WITH RESPECT TO THE SERVICES [SOURCE: STP1 OA 4.02(K)]
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the User has no obligation to pay, while the changed text states that the User has full obligation to pay. This creates a direct contradiction. Although the contradicted text does not directly specify payment obligation by the user, the change in payment responsibility introduces an inconsistency in how the agreement is interpreted and enforced, especially concerning the responsibilities outlined elsewhere in the document.
